title: lifeascode — Web App
problem: The CLI and editor extension are powerful for individual developers, but teams need a shared dashboard where anyone can browse features, view lineage, and explore the provenance of a codebase without installing anything locally. A web app makes LAC accessible to non-engineering stakeholders too.
analysis: The web-based dashboard for the LAC ecosystem. A Next.js 16 app with Supabase as the database, tRPC for type-safe API calls, and Drizzle ORM for queries. Where the CLI operates on local files in git, the web app persists features to a database and makes them accessible to anyone on the team — including non-engineers. Deployed on Vercel. The monorepo contains dedicated packages for the API router, DB schema, shared UI components, auth, and validators.
implementation: apps/nextjs/ is the Next.js App Router application. The tRPC router in packages/api/src/routers/ exposes procedures for features, search, admin, events, and GitHub integration. packages/db/ contains the Drizzle schema and migration files pointing at Supabase Postgres. packages/ui/ is a shared React component library (Tailwind + CVA + Base UI + Lucide). State is managed with Zustand (client) and TanStack Query (server). Next.js tag-based cache revalidation (revalidateTag) is used after mutations. Deployed via Vercel with config in vercel.json.

decisions.rationale: End-to-end type safety between the Next.js frontend and the API package. No separate OpenAPI spec or codegen step — types flow from the router definition to the React component.
alternativesConsidered: REST + OpenAPI — more portable but requires codegen and loses type inference
GraphQL — more flexible but heavyweight for this use case

decisions.rationale: Supabase provides a managed Postgres with realtime subscriptions and easy local dev. Drizzle gives type-safe queries without the overhead of Prisma.
alternativesConsidered: SQLite (local-only) — no multi-user or team access
Prisma — heavier, slower cold starts on Vercel edge

decisions.decision: Separate packages for api, db, ui, auth within the monorepo
decisions.rationale: Clear separation of concerns — the API package can be tested independently of the Next.js app, the UI library can be reused across multiple apps.
alternativesConsimented: Everything in apps/nextjs — faster to start but becomes unmaintainable

knownLimitations: Features in the database and features in git can desync — the web app and CLI are two separate sources of truth
Requires Supabase credentials and Vercel deployment — significantly more infrastructure than the local CLI
No offline support — all queries hit Supabase over the network
tRPC tag-based cache revalidation must be manually maintained per mutation — easy to forget and serve stale data
successCriteria: The app deploys to Vercel, connects to Supabase, and displays all features with working search, lineage view, and feature detail pages. tRPC procedures return type-safe responses with no runtime type errors.

lineage.spawnReason: The web app is the team-facing surface of the LAC ecosystem — where the CLI is for individual developers, this is for everyone else.
